human_testing_steps_covered: 1 (polyline detail), 2 (no-polyline graceful degradation), 7 (Ride It → Apple Maps)
human_testing_steps_failing: 1 (auth-gated entry from chat card), 5 (Save → Saved badge), 6 (Saved list → reopen)
human_testing_steps_uncovered: 3 (weather — jsdom only), 4 (scroll visibility — jsdom only), 8 (Android web fallback — iOS sim can't test)

flows.failure: Assertion is false: "Login with Email" is visible
flows.likely_root_cause: Flow uses `clearState: true` on launchApp (line 21) which clears the Clerk auth session. The plan-view chat cards never render because the app shows the Login screen. The `_common-auth.yaml` helper exists but is NOT invoked by `curated-route-detail.yaml` — only `_common-launch-to-plan.yaml` is run, and that helper assumes an already-authed session.
flows.fix: Either (a) remove `clearState: true` from the launchApp step, OR (b) insert a `runFlow: ../.maestro/_common-auth.yaml` step BEFORE `_common-launch-to-plan.yaml` to drive the E2E test-login button. Option (b) is more robust.

flows.failure: Assertion is false: id: save-curated-saved-badge is visible
flows.likely_root_cause: The Save button was tapped but the `save-curated-saved-badge` never appeared. Two candidates: (1) the save mutation failed silently — FU-2's `_id` return is in `buildRouteDetail` (convex/curatedRoutes.ts:345) but the live Convex dev deployment may be serving a stale function schema; verify by querying `getCuratedRoutes` and inspecting the `_id` field. (2) The Badge component renders but the testID assertion timing is too tight (Maestro waits ~30s by default; the save mutation + state update may take longer against a cold Convex connection). The testID itself is correct — confirmed at `app/(app)/curated-route/[id].tsx:466`.
flows.fix: First verify `_id` is actually returned by the live deployment (npx convex codegen + check the generated types, or query the API directly). If `_id` is present, increase the Maestro `extendedWaitUntil`/`extendedTimeout` for the saved-badge assertion.

step_gates: step 7 (Ride It → Apple Maps deep link)

flows.failure: Assertion is false: "Save", id: save-curated-button is visible
flows.likely_root_cause: Same auth-gating as `curated-route-detail`: this flow probably also lands on the Login screen instead of a saved-route detail screen, so `save-curated-button` (which only renders on the curated-route detail page at `[id].tsx:448`) is never visible. The flow needs the same `_common-auth.yaml` invocation + navigation to a curated route detail before asserting the save button.
flows.fix: Insert auth + navigation prereqs (runFlow `_common-auth.yaml` → `_common-launch-to-plan.yaml` → tap a curated card → assert on save-curated-button).

infrastructure_confirmed_working: App installs + launches on iPhone 17 sim (com.hackerpug.laneshadow)
Convex dev deployment live (FU-1 + FU-2 changes pushed during this run)
Maestro 2.5.1 drives the sim via XCUITest/WebDriverAgent (idb NOT required)
E2E test-login button + chat-input rendered (verified via discovery-smoke passing earlier)
App's polyline + graceful-degradation + maps-deep-link paths all work end-to-end
verdict_summary: The gate is RUNNABLE. 3 of 6 sprint-authored flows pass cleanly (covering human testing steps 1, 2, 7). The 3 failures cluster around two fixable root causes: (a) auth-gated flows don't invoke the `_common-auth.yaml` helper, so they land on the Login screen; (b) the save-path badge assertion may be racing the save mutation or missing a stale `_id`. Both are flow-level fixes — no app code changes implicated. Sprint 02 is NOT verified-done (3 failing flows).
